task: Add GDPR Art. 17 right-to-erasure and retention enforcement for dbo.HouseholdContacts with resident self-erase, board DSAR hard-delete, retention purge sweep, new port methods, SQL adapter DELETE methods, two new DELETE endpoints, schema migration for DepartedAt column, and full unit and integration test coverage.

key_reasoning.reason: Extends IDirectoryStore port (interface change propagates to FakeDirectoryStore and FailingDirectoryStore in Fakes.cs), adds 3 use-case classes, 2 new result type families to Ports.cs, 2 SQL adapter methods in SqlDirectoryStore, 2 endpoint methods in DirectoryEndpoints, 2 MapDelete routes in Program.cs, and a potential MarkDepartedAsync port. IDirectoryStore is the shared port contract consumed by BbqReminderService as well, making this a multi-workflow port change.

GATE-DATA-1 is explicitly open: the DPO retention period (days after departure or opt-out) is unknown and is a DPO-owned decision that cannot be engineered around. The purge trigger mechanism is an open architectural fork (admin endpoint vs BackgroundService). The departure-event signal is ambiguous (external BMS event vs IsOptedOut flag vs new DepartedAt column). Scope of 'full Art. 17 erasure' is contested — PushSubscriptions and NotificationHistory contain personal data but are declared out of scope, requiring a DPO decision. Multiple stakeholders (DPO, engineering, board) hold partially conflicting expectations.

GDPR Art. 17 is a compliance requirement where incorrect implementation creates regulatory liability, not just a quality defect. Hard DELETE is irreversible — no soft-delete safety net. R3 log-exclusion risk: erase endpoints handle HouseholdRef as a URL path parameter; no directory log-exclusion test exists (only ReservationEndpoints is covered). R2 risk on EraseMyContact: HouseholdRef must come from session only. Schema migration risk: SqlDirectoryStore.ReadRow uses ordinal-position access (columns 0–6); adding DepartedAt mid-SELECT shifts ordinals silently. PurgeExpiredContacts is the novel element — configurable cutoff, trigger mechanism, and bulk DELETE are new patterns for this codebase.

13–16 files total: 8–10 modified (Ports.cs, SqlDirectoryStore.cs, DirectoryEndpoints.cs, Program.cs, Fakes.cs, appsettings.json, db/schema.sql, SqlDirectoryStoreTests.cs, DirectoryEndpointsTests.cs, LogExclusionTests.cs) plus 5–6 new files (EraseMyContact.cs, EraseContact.cs, PurgeExpiredContacts.cs, EraseMyContactTests.cs, EraseContactTests.cs, PurgeExpiredContactsTests.cs). Schema migration file adds one more if written separately.

API layer (DirectoryEndpoints, Program.cs), Service/Application layer (Ports.cs, 3 new use cases), Repository/Adapter layer (SqlDirectoryStore), DB-Persistence layer (schema.sql DepartedAt migration). Four distinct architectural layers plus test infrastructure.

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from L to XL: GDPR Art. 17 is a compliance/security requirement — incorrect erasure creates regulatory liability
Technical Risk bumped from XL to XXL (capped): schema change (DepartedAt column) required for retention enforcement
Affected Layers bumped from L to XL: schema change adds DB-Persistence as a distinct affected layer
File Change Estimate bumped from XL to XXL (capped): schema migration (ALTER TABLE) counts as a data migration artifact requiring an additional changed file
Requirements Clarity bumped from L to XL: GATE-DATA-1 is open — retention period is undefined; purge trigger mechanism is an open architectural fork (vague acceptance criteria)
Requirements Clarity bumped from XL to XXL (capped): DPO, engineering, and board stakeholders hold partially conflicting expectations on erasure scope (PushSubscriptions in/out of scope)
Component Scope bumped from L to XL: IDirectoryStore is a core shared port contract; Fakes.cs is a shared test utility — both touched by this task
Component Scope bumped from XL to XXL (capped): BbqReminderService reads IDirectoryStore; the purge running concurrently affects multiple application workflows
split_recommendation: Split into three slices delivered in order: (1) Erasure core — EraseMyContact and EraseContact use cases with their port methods, SQL adapter single-row DELETE, two new DELETE endpoints, unit tests, integration tests, and R3 log-exclusion tests. No schema migration in this slice; DepartedAt is deferred. Routing: brainstorming. (2) Retention schema and purge logic — after GATE-DATA-1 (DPO retention period) is resolved: add DepartedAt column migration, MarkDepartedAsync port method if needed, PurgeExpiredContacts use case with configurable cutoff, integration tests for bulk DELETE. Trigger mechanism (admin endpoint vs BackgroundService) must be decided as a human gate before this slice starts. Routing: brainstorming. (3) Compliance hardening — R3 log-exclusion tests for all directory erase endpoints, PushSubscriptions erasure scope decision (ADR or documented out-of-scope), legal-basis documentation for financial ledger retention under Art. 17(3)(b). Routing: writing-plans (low code, high governance). Do not start slice 2 until GATE-DATA-1 is closed.
